sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Juventus are in strong form, having won 4 of their last 5 matches, including a recent 2-0 victory over Pisa. Lecce, on the other hand, have struggled, managing only 2 wins in their last 5 games, with a heavy 0-3 loss to Como in their most recent outing. This disparity in form gives Juventus a clear edge going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Juventus have dominated Lecce, securing a 2-1 win in April 2025 and a 1-1 draw in December 2024. Juventus have not lost to Lecce in their last 10 meetings, often scoring first, which suggests they hold a psychological advantage in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Juventus, sitting 5th in the league with 32 points, boast a solid attack (23 goals scored) and a decent defense (15 conceded), ranking well in big chances created (3rd). Lecce, positioned 16th with 16 points, have struggled offensively (11 goals scored) and defensively (22 conceded), highlighting their vulnerability against a stronger side like Juventus.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Juventus’ Kenan Yıldız has been in fine form, averaging a 7.58 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 2 goals and 2 assists, though they’ll miss key striker Dušan Vlahović. Lecce are without several important players like Medon Berisha, impacting their midfield creativity, and lack standout performers in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Juventus’ superior form, historical dominance, and home advantage at Allianz Stadium, they are heavily favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 65% probability for a home victory. Lecce’s defensive frailties and poor away form make an upset unlikely, though they may aim to keep the scoreline tight.
